---
title: "historic_districts_exploration"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
# Load packages
library(tidyverse)
library(tidygeocoder)
library(janitor)
library(tidytext)
library(leaflet)
library(tigris)
```

```{r}
# Cleaning the historic_districts dataset so only the columns we want are present (add later)
```
--

```{r}
# Import cleaned (sort of) dataset
historic_districts <- read_csv("../data/Historic Districts/historic_districts_clean2.csv")

# Mapping w/ leaflet!
prelim_map <- leaflet(data = historic_districts) %>%
   addTiles() %>%
   addCircleMarkers(~longitude, ~latitude, popup = ~property_name, radius = 3)
prelim_map
```

```{r}

# Mapping w/ leaflet!
prelim_map2 <- leaflet(data = historic_districts) %>%
   addTiles() %>%
   addMarkers(clusterOptions = markerClusterOptions(), popup = ~property_name)
  #addCircleMarkers(~longitude, ~latitude, popup = ~property_name, radius = 3)
prelim_map2
```
```{r}
# Now I want to do the chloropleth by area by state (?) -- group by state
```


640 acres in 1 mile

```{r}
# Actually what I want is to group by state and then ...

# MAYBE SHOULD JOIN DATA FOR THE TOTAL ACREAGE OF THE WHOLE STATE TO STANDARDIZE 
# ^ for this should find the area of each state and then divide the acreage by that...

by_state <- historic_districts %>% 
  group_by(state) %>% 
  summarise(total_acreage = sum(acreage_of_property, na.rm=TRUE),
            total_num_districts = n())

```


Chloropleth map for # of acres of districts

```{r}
# 
# acreage_of_property na.rm = TRUE --> ignore missing values

```


```{r}
# Get state geometries
states_sf <- tigris::states(cb = TRUE, resolution = "20m")
# cb = TRUE simplfies the file
# resolution = 20m is the lowest
# this gives 2024 data
```
```{r}
# Join data to shapefile
map_data <- states_sf %>% 
  left_join(by_state, by = c("NAME" = "state"))
```
```{r}
# choosing the var

#explore_var <- map_data$total_num_districts
```

```{r}
# Create a color palette for the map
# might want to change this to binning or quantile or something

my_palette <- colorNumeric(
  palette = "viridis", 
  domain = map_data$total_num_districts,
  na.color = "transparent"
)
```
```{r}
leaflet(map_data) %>% 
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") %>% 
  addPolygons(
    fillColor = ~my_palette(total_num_districts),
    fillOpacity = .75,
    color = "white", # border color
    weight = 1,
    smoothFactor = 0.5, # slightly crisper borders -- default is 1 (higher values > more simplification > jaggier borders but shorter rendering)
    # add the highlight/hover and tooltip things
  ) %>% 
  addLegend(
    pal = my_palette,
    value = map_data$total_num_districts, # same as values   = ~total_num_districts
    position = "bottomright",
    title = "# of historic districts"
  )
```


```{r}
# Create a color palette for the map
# might want to change this to binning or quantile or something

my_palette <- colorNumeric(
  palette = "viridis", 
  domain = map_data$total_acreage,
  na.color = "transparent"
)
```
```{r}
leaflet(map_data) %>% 
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") %>% 
  addPolygons(
    fillColor = ~my_palette(total_acreage),
    fillOpacity = .75,
    color = "white", # border color
    weight = 1,
    smoothFactor = 0.5, # slightly crisper borders -- default is 1 (higher values > more simplification > jaggier borders but shorter rendering)
    # add the highlight/hover and tooltip things
  ) %>% 
  addLegend(
    pal = my_palette,
    value = map_data$total_acreage, # same as values   = ~total_num_districts
    position = "bottomright",
    title = "acreage of historic districts"
  )
```







---------------------

```{r}
# Loading historic landmarks data for historic districts!
historic_landmarks <- read_csv("../data/Historic Districts/Raw/csv_RAW_national-register-listed_20260522.csv") # .. means one folder up
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidygeocoder)
library(janitor)
library(tidytext)
library(leaflet)
library(lubridate)
library(arcgisgeocode)
```

```{r}
# Filtering the historic landmarks dataset to make sure that only historic districts are present
historic_districts <- historic_landmarks %>% 
  clean_names() %>% 
  mutate(listed_date = mdy(listed_date),
         nhl_designated_date = mdy(nhl_designated_date),
         across(where(is.character) & -c(prefix, street_number, external_link, other_names), str_to_title),
         district_name = property_name) %>%
  filter(str_detect(district_name, "Historic District")) %>% 
  mutate(district_name = str_remove(district_name, " Historic District")) %>% 
  mutate(address = paste(property_name, street_number, city, state, sep=", "))
```
```{r}
# Adding location

# Have to split because batch limit is 10,000 and there are 12,337
historic_districts_clean_L <- historic_districts[1:200,] %>% 
  geocode(address, method = 'arcgis', lat = latitude , long = longitude)

```
